_comment: Tester-program task catalog (ORDER 018). Committed JSON is the source of truth for WHAT can be tested; live claim/slot state lives in the SQLite store. status: open | coming-soon | closed. Every task is a REAL fleet product — coming-soon tasks say honestly why they are not open yet. guided-walkthrough tasks additionally carry a steps array (title, instruction, look_for, question) that drives the /testing/s/{token}/guide flow.

tasks.title: Review the SuperBot public site
tasks.brief: Browse the SuperBot marketing and reference site the way a curious Discord server owner would. Open every page in the header nav (Features, Commands, Games, Changelog, Status), open at least five command detail pages, try the search palette (Cmd/Ctrl+K), and toggle the light/dark theme. Look for: links that 404 or go nowhere, pages that fail to load or load slowly, copy that is confusing or reads unfinished, layout problems on a phone-sized screen, and anything that would make you distrust the site. Note the exact page URL for every issue you report.

tasks.title: Review the SuperBot dashboard
tasks.brief: Explore the read-only SuperBot dashboard end to end. Visit every section reachable from its navigation, including the /admin area (it is an openly labeled dry-run — nothing you do there controls anything real). Look for: panels or tables that render empty or broken, numbers or labels that make no sense without insider knowledge, error messages after refreshing or using the browser back button, mobile rendering problems, and dead links. Tell us where you got lost first — the first confusing screen is the most valuable finding.

tasks.title: Review the control-plane /projects pages
tasks.brief: Start at /projects and click through every project card and its detail/dispatch views. You are seeing an internal fleet-operations surface — judge it as an outsider. Look for: navigation that traps you or loses your place, jargon a first-time visitor cannot decode, links that 404, pages slower than ~3 seconds, and layout breakage on mobile. List each term you did not understand and each click that did not do what you expected.

tasks.title: Review the program-review site
tasks.brief: Read the program-review site as a skeptical outside reviewer: the process pages, growth charts, successes, the problems page, the fleet index, and the review editions feed. Look for: claims that are unclear or unsupported, charts that are hard to read, broken links, and pages that render badly on mobile.
tasks.note: Honest hold: the review service's public URL is not yet confirmed live (the Railway service still needs an owner click; the URL above is the expected one, unverified). This task opens when the URL is verified.

tasks.title: Playtest Lumen Drift
tasks.brief: Play Lumen Drift for at least 30 minutes across several sessions. Look for: crashes or freezes, states where you get stuck with no way forward, controls that feel wrong or are never explained, difficulty spikes, and moments you got bored — say exactly when and why. Note your device and browser.
tasks.note: Activates when the game deploys — Lumen Drift is not deployed yet, so there is honestly nothing to test today.

tasks.title: Playtest mineverse
tasks.brief: Play mineverse for at least 45 minutes. Try to break it: rapid clicks, leaving mid-action, refreshing at odd moments. Look for: progress that fails to save, unclear goals in the first five minutes, UI elements that overlap or vanish on smaller screens, and anything that made you want to quit. Note your device and browser.
tasks.note: Activates when the game deploys — mineverse is not deployed yet, so there is honestly nothing to test today.

tasks.title: Playtest the games-web arcade
tasks.brief: Work through every game in the arcade for a few minutes each. Look for: games that fail to start, broken or laggy input, scores that do not record, confusing instructions, and mobile/touch problems. Rank the games from most to least fun and say why in one line each.
tasks.note: Activates when the game deploys — the games-web arcade is not deployed yet, so there is honestly nothing to test today.

tasks.title: Guided walkthrough: first visit to the SuperBot site
tasks.brief: A step-by-step guided review of the SuperBot public site. After you claim the task, your private guide page walks you through six screens, one at a time, each with an instruction, things to look for, and one question to answer. An optional AI guide sits beside the steps: you can chat with it, and — only if you explicitly opt in — share your screen so it can ask about what it sees. The steps work fully without the AI.

steps.title: Homepage first impression
steps.instruction: Open the site's homepage in another tab or window and look at it for about ten seconds before scrolling.
steps.look_for: Whether you can tell what SuperBot is, who it is for, and what the page wants you to do next — without scrolling or clicking.
steps.question: In one or two sentences: what is this site offering, and what did it seem to want you to do first? If you couldn't tell, say so.

steps.title: Features
steps.instruction: Open Features from the header navigation and click into one feature that interests you.
steps.look_for: Cards that render empty or broken, copy that reads unfinished, and whether the feature's detail page delivers what its card promised.
steps.question: Which feature did you open, and did its detail page answer your questions about it? What was missing or confusing?

steps.title: Commands and search
steps.instruction: Open Commands, then press Cmd/Ctrl+K (or the search button) and search for any command you saw.
steps.look_for: Whether the search palette opens, whether its results make sense, and whether command detail pages load and read clearly.
steps.question: Did search find what you typed? Name one command page you opened and one thing on it that was unclear.

steps.title: Games and Changelog
steps.instruction: Visit the Games page, then the Changelog page.
steps.look_for: Empty or broken sections, dates or entries that look wrong, and whether the changelog tells you the project is alive.
steps.question: After these two pages, does the project feel actively maintained? Point at what convinced you either way.

steps.title: Status page
steps.instruction: Open the Status page from the navigation.
steps.look_for: Whether the systems list renders, whether the statuses are understandable to an outsider, and anything that looks stuck or fake.
steps.question: Do you trust what the status page tells you? What would make it more convincing?

steps.title: Theme and phone width
steps.instruction: Toggle the light/dark theme button in the header, then narrow your browser window to roughly phone width (or use your phone) and revisit two pages you already saw.
steps.look_for: Unreadable text after the theme switch, overlapping or clipped layout at phone width, and navigation that becomes unusable.
steps.question: What broke or got ugly in the other theme or at phone width? Name the page and what you saw — or say it held up.
